title: thymophylla neomexicana
intro: Thymophylla neomexicana, also known as New Mexico dwarf cineraria, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the daisy family. It is native to the southwestern United States and is often used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Thymophylla neomexicana include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for colds and flu. Thymophylla neomexicana can be prepared for herbal use through teas, infusions, and tinctures, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized in a variety of ways.
